title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast explores whether AI systems are susceptible to social engineering attacks, covering prompt injection vulnerabilities, self-replicating AI worms, and the emerging risk of nonhuman identity compromises in enterprise security. Discusses whether AI agents are more gullible than humans and examines the operational impact of inadequate credential auditing for nonhuman identities.
writeup: IBM Security Intelligence examines social engineering attacks aimed at AI systems, covering prompt injection observed at Meta and Instagram alongside self-replicating AI worms. The discussion weighs whether AI agents are more vulnerable to manipulation than humans, a question that matters as enterprises deploy agents with real access and authority. A central concern is the security risk from unaudited nonhuman identities in enterprise environments, where machine and agent credentials proliferate without oversight.
